metadata.description: Production-grade survival probability parameters for EMERGI-ENV simulation. Covers 42 medical condition categories, 15 decay model types, pre-hospital intervention impacts, age/comorbidity modifiers, RPM correlation matrices, and MCI triage modifiers. Calibrated against AHA ACLS 2020, NICE Emergency Guidelines, Indian EMS Standards, WHO Emergency Care Guidelines, and EMRI 108 Maharashtra outcome data.
data_sources: National Poison Control Centre India Data
metadata.reward_integration_note: Graders integrate survival probability P(t) over episode timesteps. Each step = 3 simulation minutes. Reward = sum(P(t_i)) * outcome_weight — not binary dispatched/not. Agent must minimise cumulative survival probability loss.
metadata.golden_hour_definition: The time window within which definitive care must begin for optimal survival. Not literally 60 minutes — condition-specific. STEMI=90min, Stroke=270min, Polytrauma=60min, Burns=120min.

exponential.description: Standard exponential decay. Fastest initial drop, asymptotic floor. Used for: cardiac arrest, anaphylaxis, major haemorrhage.

linear.description: Constant rate of deterioration. Used for: polytrauma, burns, sepsis.

sigmoid.description: Slow initial decline, steep cliff at midpoint, then plateau. Used for: STEMI (90min cliff), stroke (thrombolysis window).

step_cliff.formula: P(t) = P_before if t < cliff_time else P_after; with linear interpolation across cliff_width
step_cliff.description: Relatively stable then sudden severe drop. Used for: obstructed airway, tension pneumothorax, ruptured AAA.

bimodal.formula: P(t) = phase1_curve(t) if t < phase_transition else phase2_curve(t - phase_transition)
bimodal.description: Two distinct phases of deterioration. Used for: severe TBI (initial bleed + secondary injury), crush syndrome (early fluid loss + delayed rhabdomyolysis).

plateau_then_drop.formula: P(t) = survival_at_zero if t < plateau_end else exponential_decay(t - plateau_end)
plateau_then_drop.description: Compensated state then decompensation. Used for: ectopic pregnancy, slow bleeds, diabetic emergencies.

rapid_initial.formula: P(t) = survival_floor + (survival_at_zero - survival_floor) * exp(-rapid_decay_rate * min(t, rapid_phase_end)) * exp(-slow_decay_rate * max(0, t - rapid_phase_end))
rapid_initial.description: Very fast initial drop then slower sustained decline. Used for: VF cardiac arrest (10% per minute), neonatal asphyxia.

treatment_responsive.formula: P_untreated(t) for no treatment; P_treated(t) = recovery_factor * P_untreated(t) if treated within treatment_window
treatment_responsive.description: Survival probability has two paths depending on whether correct pre-hospital treatment given. Used for: hypoglycaemia, anaphylaxis, organophosphate.

stemi_anterior.cliff_annotation: Steep drop at 90 min — LAD territory, large anterior wall at risk. D2B target 90 min.
stemi_anterior.simulation_notes: Anterior STEMI highest-mortality STEMI type. LAD occlusion. VF most common complication. Cath lab activation from dispatch is critical reward signal.

stemi_inferior.cliff_annotation: RCA territory. RV infarction risk — avoid nitrates if RV involved. Generally better prognosis than anterior.
stemi_inferior.simulation_notes: Inferior STEMI often presents atypically (nausea, vomiting). RV infarction in 30-50%. AV block risk. Better mortality than anterior.

stemi_with_vf_arrest.cliff_annotation: VF cardiac arrest — 10% survival loss per minute without CPR. Time is absolute muscle.
stemi_with_vf_arrest.simulation_notes: Highest-acuity STEMI. Every dispatch second counts. AED + CPR chain of survival critical. Post-ROSC cath lab = massive bonus.

stemi_cocaine.simulation_notes: CRITICAL: Beta-blockers contraindicated — unopposed alpha causes severe hypertension. Wrong drug selection is severe penalty.

stemi_post_cabg.simulation_notes: Graft thrombosis more likely than native vessel. Higher procedural complexity. Worse outcomes than de-novo STEMI.

ischemic_stroke.cliff_annotation: Thrombolysis window 4.5h, thrombectomy 24h. Every 15 min = 1% absolute mortality increase.

ischemic_stroke_wake_up.simulation_notes: MRI capability absolutely required. Thrombolysis eligibility determined by DWI-FLAIR mismatch. Wrong hospital wastes critical time.

hemorrhagic_stroke_sah.cliff_annotation: Re-bleeding risk peaks in first 24h — 15-20% re-bleed rate. Vasospasm days 4-14.

paediatric_stroke.simulation_notes: Rare. Paediatric neurology + cardiac centre required. Thrombolysis NOT routine. SCD patients: exchange transfusion.

polytrauma_blunt.cliff_annotation: Linear deterioration. Haemorrhage and traumatic brain injury drive mortality. Time to OR is critical.
polytrauma_blunt.simulation_notes: Damage control resuscitation. Scoop and run preferred over stay and play. Level 1 trauma = massive outcome difference.

severe_tbi.simulation_notes: Bimodal: primary injury + secondary brain swelling. ICP management critical. Hypotension/hypoxia double secondary injury.

skull_base_fracture.display_name: Basal Skull Fracture with Brain Injury

crush_syndrome.simulation_notes: Pre-treat rhabdomyolysis BEFORE extrication. IV fluids + alkalinisation mandatory. AKI will develop — dialysis centre needed.

chest_trauma.display_name: Severe Chest Trauma — Multiple Rib Fractures / Haemopneumothorax

paediatric_respiratory.display_name: Severe Paediatric Respiratory Emergency (Bronchiolitis / Epiglottitis / Croup)
paediatric_respiratory.simulation_notes: Epiglottitis: DO NOT examine throat. Rapid deterioration possible. Paediatric anaesthesia for controlled airway.

decompression_sickness.simulation_notes: Only HBO treats this. Oxygen 100% pre-hospital. Wrong hospital (no HBO) = massive penalty.

burns_electrical.cliff_annotation: Arrhythmia risk peaks in first 30 min. After 24h monitoring normal sinus = discharge safe.

severe_malaria.display_name: Severe Falciparum Malaria with Organ Failure

weil_disease.display_name: Weil's Disease — Severe Leptospirosis with MOF

dengue_severe.display_name: Severe Dengue with Plasma Leakage / Shock

anaphylaxis.simulation_notes: Adrenaline is life-saving. Every minute of delay = survival loss. Biphasic reaction risk means 6h observation mandatory.

submersion.simulation_notes: Don't pronounce dead until warm and unresponsive. Cold water drowning has much better prognosis.

organophosphate_poisoning.simulation_notes: SLUDGE syndrome. Large atropine doses. Pralidoxime early. PPE for crew — organophosphate absorbed through skin.

snakebite_neurotoxic.cliff_annotation: Respiratory paralysis develops. Ventilator needed after cliff. Airway management critical.

obstetric_hemorrhage.display_name: Major Obstetric Haemorrhage — PPH / Abruption / Ectopic

cardiac_arrest_vf.simulation_notes: Chain of survival: Early recognition → Early CPR → Early defibrillation → Post-resuscitation care. Each link = survival multiplier.

hyperkalaemia.display_name: Hyperkalaemia with Cardiac Arrest / Arrest Risk
hyperkalaemia.cliff_annotation: Sine wave pattern = imminent arrest. Calcium gluconate is membrane stabiliser — immediate.
hyperkalaemia.simulation_notes: MICU mandatory — transcardiac pacing capability and calcium gluconate. Dialysis = only definitive treatment.

heat_stroke.cliff_annotation: Cooling within 30 min target. Hot dry skin = no sweating = loss of thermoregulation.

aaa_rupture.simulation_notes: 80% mortality without surgery. Permissive hypotension is survival. Massive fluid = death.

mci_rta.simulation_notes: START triage applies. Spread casualties across hospitals. Multi-agency required. Over-requesting mutual aid is penalised.

blast_injury.blast_triad_note: Barotrauma + fragmentation + blunt — all three injuries possible

minor_trauma.simulation_notes: P3 condition. BLS adequate. No urgent intervention needed.

expected_death.simulation_notes: P0/Expectant. BLS for confirmation and family support. No aggressive resuscitation.

rpm_survival_correlation.description: START triage RPM scoring correlation to survival probability modifier. Applied on top of condition-specific curve.

age_modifiers.description: Applied multiplicatively to base survival probability. Age affects baseline resilience and comorbidity burden.

neonatal_0_28_days.note: Neonatal physiology. Rapid deterioration. Specialised care required.

infant_1_12_months.note: High risk for serious illness. Paediatric ED mandatory.

elderly_above_80.note: Frailty. Polypharmacy. Goals of care discussion warranted.

comorbidity_modifiers.description: Applied additively as survival probability adjustments. Multiple comorbidities stack.

diabetes_mellitus.note: Impaired healing, atypical presentations, immune dysfunction.

chronic_kidney_disease.note: Drug dose adjustment, fluid balance issues.

environmental_modifiers.description: Contextual factors affecting survival independent of medical condition.

night_time_00_to_06.note: Reduced crew alertness, reduced bystander help.

mci_scene_secondary_hazard.note: Active hazard reduces speed of access.

tribal_area_no_prehospital_care.note: No pre-hospital care before ambulance arrival.

pre_hospital_intervention_library.description: Universal intervention impact library. Condition-specific overrides in each condition block. These are defaults.

protocol_compliance_scoring.description: Graders check protocol adherence beyond raw outcome. Compliance adds up to +0.15 bonus per episode.

als_for_stroke.rule: Stroke dispatch must use ALS minimum

nearest_level1_trauma_within_30_min.rule: Major trauma must go to nearest Level 1 trauma centre within 30 minutes

no_routing_to_diverted_hospital.rule: Do not route to hospital on diversion

multi_agency_for_trapped_victim.rule: Trapped victim requires Police + Fire + EMS

cath_lab_activation_for_stemi.rule: STEMI dispatch must trigger cath lab pre-notification

stroke_unit_for_stroke.rule: Stroke patient must go to hospital with stroke unit

burns_unit_for_major_burns.rule: Burns >20% BSA must go to burns unit

paediatric_ed_for_paediatric_emergency.rule: Paediatric emergencies require paediatric ED capability

noop_on_unknown_unit_penalty.rule: Must not noop() on unit with communication failure unless within 2 steps of contact

start_triage_in_mci.rule: MCI with 5+ patients requires START triage protocol

mutual_aid_in_surge.rule: Task 9 surge requires mutual aid declaration within 5 steps

mci_triage_modifiers.description: Applied during Task 7 (MCI) and Task 9 (surge). START protocol scoring.

immediate_red.rpm_criteria: Respirations present AND (RR>30 OR RR<10) OR Pulse absent (radial) OR Mental status altered

delayed_yellow.definition: Serious but stable. Can wait 30-60 minutes.
delayed_yellow.rpm_criteria: Respirations normal AND Pulse present AND Mental status alert — but cannot walk

expectant_black.definition: Unsurvivable injuries or deceased. Resources better used elsewhere.
expectant_black.rpm_criteria: No respirations after airway opening attempt OR combinations indicating unsurvivable

hospital_spread_bonus.description: Bonus for distributing MCI patients across multiple hospitals

resource_conservation_bonus.description: Bonus for not using MICU on P3/Minimal patients

reward_calculation.survival_probability_delta: P(t+1) - P(t) — negative means patient deteriorating
reward_calculation.normalization_factor: based on max theoretical reward given task difficulty
